title: Add file versioning with content-hash dedup and per-version metadata
business_context: Document storage stores files but no version history. Users want to see prior versions and restore. Need versioning with content-hash dedup so reuploading same file doesn't double storage.
constraints: Restore version creates a new latest version
reasoning: FileContent stored by sha256 (dedup). File document holds metadata + array of versions referencing FileContent by hash. New upload computes hash, creates content if new, appends version. Restore creates new version pointing at old content.

after_state.description: Content-addressed dedup + versions array + restore via new version.

files.purpose: Content table keyed by hash with refCount
File metadata with versions array
Service computes hash, dedups storage, appends version, supports restore
Version list with restore button

migration_strategy: Step 1: Add FileContent collection and versions field
Step 2: Migrate uploads to compute hash and append version
Step 3: Backfill existing files as v0
Step 5: Add GC for FileContent with refCount=0
testing_strategy: Dedup test: upload same file twice, assert one FileContent row with refCount=2. Restore test: restore v0, assert new version appears with same contentHash. GC test: delete file, assert refCount decrements; at 0 content removed. Use a production-shaped fixture with seeded tenant/user/data records. CI runs this as a deterministic regression suite for fs-101 with failure artifacts attached.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A naive solution for Add file versioning with content-hash dedup and per-version metadata handles the demo path but misses the feature addition with refactor boundary: authorization, persistence, reconnect or retry behavior, rollback, and render commit time are not proved under production-shaped fixtures.

title: Replace whole-document writes with $set patches to reduce IO and reduce conflict
business_context: Service uses Mongoose findById, mutate, save - sends full doc on each update. Causes write churn and conflicts under concurrent updates. Need surgical $set on changed fields only.
constraints: Concurrent updates to different fields must not conflict
reasoning: Switch to findOneAndUpdate with $set for partial updates. Use $inc for counters. Validation via runValidators option. Conflict between concurrent updates to different fields disappears.

after_state.description: Patch-based $set updates with $inc for counters.

files.purpose: $set for partial updates + $inc for atomic counters

migration_strategy: Step 1: Migrate one service at a time
Step 3: Lint rule discouraging findById+save pattern
testing_strategy: Use a fixture for Replace whole-document writes with $set patches to reduce IO and reduce conflict that exercises the owned boundary, then add: happy-path test, unauthorized/invalid-input test, retry or concurrency simulation, persistence/contract assertion, and rollback/regression test. Assert the observable output, database/cache side effect, emitted event or log, and one failure metric. Run the suite in CI with deterministic seeds and a production-shaped fixture so fs-102 cannot pass through a mocked happy path only.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A naive solution for Replace whole-document writes with $set patches to reduce IO and reduce conflict handles the demo path but misses the performance crisis boundary: authorization, persistence, reconnect or retry behavior, rollback, and state drift incidents are not proved under production-shaped fixtures.

title: Add real-time delivery tracking from Socket.IO server to mobile push when offline
business_context: Order status updates pushed via Socket.IO when user is on web. Mobile users miss updates if app is closed. Need fallback to push notifications when socket inactive.
constraints: Must not duplicate notification across web + push
Push only when user has no active socket within last 60s
reasoning: Track active socket presence per user in Redis with 60s TTL. On notify, check presence; if absent, enqueue push job. Worker calls FCM/APNs. User preferences gate the push.

after_state.description: Presence-aware delivery with fallback to push when socket absent.

files.purpose: Presence-aware routing; push only when socket inactive and prefs allow
Sockets renew presence every 30s while connected
Worker dispatches to platform-specific push services

migration_strategy: Step 1: Add presence tracking and push fallback
Step 3: Validate dedup - one notification per event
testing_strategy: Online test: socket active, assert socket emit only. Offline: no socket, assert push enqueued. Dedup: send 5 events, count delivered = 5 across channels not 10. Use a production-shaped fixture with seeded tenant/user/data records. CI runs this as a deterministic regression suite for fs-103 with failure artifacts attached.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A naive solution for Add real-time delivery tracking from Socket.IO server to mobile push when offline handles the demo path but misses the realtime e2e boundary: authorization, persistence, reconnect or retry behavior, rollback, and policy block rate are not proved under production-shaped fixtures.

title: Add timing-safe token comparison and constant-time email lookup to defeat timing attacks
business_context: Password reset and email lookup endpoints leak existence via timing - finding email in DB is faster than not. Need constant-time response.
constraints: Variance must be small enough to defeat timing oracle
Database query time still varies - acceptable but capped

files.content: export const forgotPassword = async (req, res, next) => { const user = await UserModel.findOne({ email: req.body.email }); if (user) { /* send email */ } res.json({ ok: true }); };

reasoning: Always perform a constant-cost operation regardless of whether user exists: do the bcrypt with a fake password against a dummy hash so timing is roughly constant. Use crypto.timingSafeEqual for token comparison. Pad response with random delay.

after_state.description: Constant-cost constant-cost negative-path hash verification and jittered response padding + timing-safe token compare + small random jitter.

files.purpose: Helpers for constant-time comparisons and constant-cost negative-path hash verification and jittered response padding

title: Add asset preview generator with offline rendering pipeline
business_context: 3D asset library needs thumbnail previews for each GLB. Generating in browser on every list view is wasteful. Need background worker that renders thumbnails offline using node-canvas or headless GPU.
reasoning: On asset upload, enqueue thumbnail job. Worker uses three-render-pipeline-offscreen with EGL or, if not available, a headless browser via puppeteer running a tiny three.js scene. Resulting PNG uploaded to S3, URL stored on asset.

after_state.description: Async thumbnail generation via worker + S3 upload.

files.purpose: Worker renders thumbnail in a long-lived headless browser and uploads
Upload triggers thumbnail job

files.content:
<!doctype html>
<html>
<body style="margin:0">
<canvas id="c" width="512" height="512"></canvas>
<script type="module">
import * as THREE from 'three';
import { GLTFLoader } from 'three/examples/jsm/loaders/GLTFLoader.js';
const url = new URLSearchParams(location.search).get('model');
const renderer = new THREE.WebGLRenderer({ canvas: document.getElementById('c'), alpha: true });
const scene = new THREE.Scene();
scene.add(new THREE.AmbientLight(0xffffff, 0.6));
const light = new THREE.DirectionalLight(0xffffff, 0.8); light.position.set(3,5,3); scene.add(light);
const camera = new THREE.PerspectiveCamera(35, 1, 0.1, 100);
camera.position.set(2,1.5,2); camera.lookAt(0,0,0);
new GLTFLoader().load(url, (gltf) => { scene.add(gltf.scene); renderer.render(scene, camera); window.renderDone = true; });
</script>
</body>
</html>
files.purpose: Page rendered headless to produce thumbnail screenshot

migration_strategy: Step 3: Backfill thumbnails for existing assets

Assert the observable output, database/cache side effect, emitted event or log, and one failure metric. Run the suite in CI with deterministic seeds and a production-shaped fixture so fs-120 cannot pass through a mocked happy path only.

title: Add CAPTCHA fallback after suspicious activity without affecting normal users
business_context: Bot signups spike. Adding CAPTCHA to all signups frustrates legit users. Need risk-based CAPTCHA - shown only after failed attempts or signals.
constraints: Cannot break mobile clients without CAPTCHA support

files.content: export const signup = async (req: any, res: any, next: any) => { /* always processes */ };

reasoning: Risk signals: many signups from same IP in short window, missing/known-bot user-agent, prior failures. Endpoint returns 403 with { captchaRequired: true } when risk exceeds threshold; client renders Turnstile/hCaptcha widget, retries with token. Server verifies token with provider.

title: Migrate from prop-drilling theme variables to CSS variables consumed via Tailwind
business_context: Theme passed via React Context everywhere - performance hit and verbose. Move to CSS vars set on root, Tailwind tokens reference them, components stay clean.
reasoning: Set CSS vars on :root from JS once. Tailwind config maps tokens to var(). Components use Tailwind classes. Theme switcher updates vars.

after_state.description: CSS-var driven theme; Tailwind tokens; no prop drilling.

title: Add 2FA with TOTP, recovery codes, and per-device trust
business_context: Add 2FA option. Need TOTP enrollment, recovery codes, and 'trust this device for 30 days' to reduce friction.

files.content: export const login = async (req: any, res: any) => { /* password only */ };

reasoning: Use otplib for TOTP. Enroll: generate secret, show QR (otpauth URL), verify with code. Recovery codes hashed in DB, single-use. Trusted device: random token stored hashed + per-device entry; cookie on client. On login: if 2FA enabled and no trusted device cookie, prompt for code; verify TOTP or recovery; on success, optionally issue trusted cookie.

after_state.description: TOTP enrollment + recovery codes + trusted device cookie.

title: Reduce frontend bundle by deferring polyfills, splitting vendor, and dropping moment for date-fns
business_context: Bundle is 2.4MB. Moment is 320KB, polyfills another 200KB for browsers we no longer support. Need substantial bundle reduction.

files.content: import moment from 'moment'; export function DateView({ ts }: { ts: string }) { return {moment(ts).format('LL')}; }
module.exports = { presets: [['@babel/preset-env', { targets: 'ie 11' }]] };

reasoning: Switch to date-fns (tree-shakeable). Update browserslist to last 2 versions. Audit vendor chunk via webpack-bundle-analyzer; split common vendors and large libs into their own chunks.

after_state.description: date-fns, modern browser target, vendor chunk split.

files.content: import { format, parseISO } from 'date-fns'; export function DateView({ ts }: { ts: string }) { return {format(parseISO(ts), 'PPP')}; }
files.purpose: date-fns - only the format and parse functions ship

files.content:
last 2 versions
not dead
not ie 11
